introduction: basic_svc is a minimal HTTP ingestion service that receives audit events from agent-fox instances via POST and persists them to a local embedded SQLite database, serving as a concrete, end-to-end verifiable integration target for agent-fox tooling and spec validation.

glossary.AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN: Environment variable holding the static shared secret used for bearer token authentication; required with no default value.
glossary.Bearer <token>: The exact Authorization header format required for authentication; the prefix 'Bearer ' (capital B, single space) is mandatory.
glossary.DB_PATH: Environment variable specifying the file path for the SQLite database; defaults to ./data/events.db.
glossary.Echo Framework: The Go HTTP framework used to implement routing, middleware, and error handling for this service.
glossary.GET /healthz: Liveness probe endpoint; returns 200 OK when the service process is running.
glossary.GET /readyz: Readiness probe endpoint; verifies database availability via a lightweight SQL ping and returns 200 OK or 503 Service Unavailable.
glossary.LOG_LEVEL: Environment variable controlling logging verbosity; accepts debug, info, warn, or error; defaults to info.
glossary.PORT: Environment variable specifying the HTTP listen port; defaults to 8080.
glossary.POST /v1/events: The HTTP endpoint for ingesting audit events; accepts a JSON body and a valid bearer token, storing the raw event in SQLite and returning 201 Created.
glossary.SELECT 1: A lightweight SQL query used as a database ping to verify connectivity in the readiness probe.
glossary.SIGINT: Unix signal (Ctrl-C) used to initiate graceful shutdown during local development.
glossary.SIGTERM: Unix signal sent by Kubernetes to initiate graceful pod termination.
glossary.af: Short form of agent-fox; the CLI tooling that drives audit event ingestion and spec validation.
glossary.agent-fox: The tooling suite (also abbreviated af) that sends audit events to this service and exercises its integration pipeline.
glossary.events: The single SQLite table that stores ingested audit event payloads along with a UUID primary key and receipt timestamp.
glossary.modernc.org/sqlite: The pure-Go SQLite driver used for embedded database access without CGO.
glossary.payload: The TEXT column in the events table that stores the raw JSON body of an ingested audit event.
glossary.received_at: The DATETIME column in the events table that is automatically set to the current UTC time when an event is inserted.

user_story.goal: POST an audit event to the service with a valid bearer token and JSON body
user_story.benefit: the event is persisted in SQLite and I receive a 201 Created confirmation

acceptance_criteria.action: store the raw JSON body in the events table and return 201 Created with an empty response body and no Content-Type header
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 Created; empty response body; no Content-Type header set on the response
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST /v1/events request is received with a valid Authorization header, Content-Type: application/json, and a non-empty body

acceptance_criteria.action: accept and store the raw JSON body of a POST /v1/events request as-is without performing any schema validation beyond confirming the body is non-empty
generate a UUID for the id column and set received_at to the current UTC time on every insert into the events table

edge_cases.action: reject the request and return 400 Bad Request with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400 Bad Request; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json
edge_cases.error_condition: the Content-Type header is absent or is not application/json

edge_cases.action: reject the request and return 400 Bad Request with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400 Bad Request; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json

edge_cases.action: return 500 Internal Server Error with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json
edge_cases.error_condition: a database or internal error occurs while storing the event

user_story.goal: restrict POST /v1/events to callers presenting the correct static bearer token
user_story.benefit: unauthorised clients cannot ingest events into the database

acceptance_criteria.action: extract the bearer token from the Authorization header using exactly the format 'Bearer <token>' (capital B, single space) and compare it against the value of AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN
allow the request to proceed to payload validation and storage

edge_cases.action: reject the request and return 401 Unauthorized with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json

edge_cases.action: reject the request and return 401 Unauthorized with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json
edge_cases.error_condition: the Authorization header is present but does not begin with exactly 'Bearer ' (capital B, single space), has extra whitespace, or is otherwise malformed

edge_cases.action: reject the request and return 401 Unauthorized with an Echo Framework error body
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 401 Unauthorized; body: {"message": "<error description>"}; Content-Type: application/json
edge_cases.error_condition: the Authorization header is well-formed but the token value does not match AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN

user_story.goal: probe liveness and readiness of basic_svc
user_story.benefit: unhealthy pods are restarted and traffic is only routed to ready instances

acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET /readyz request is received and the SELECT 1 query against the database succeeds

edge_cases.action: return 503 Service Unavailable with body {"message": "service unavailable"}
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 503 Service Unavailable; body: {"message": "service unavailable"}; Content-Type: application/json
edge_cases.error_condition: the SELECT 1 query fails or the database connection is unavailable when GET /readyz is received

user_story.goal: configure basic_svc entirely through environment variables
user_story.benefit: the service can be deployed in different environments without code changes

acceptance_criteria.action: read PORT (default 8080), DB_PATH (default ./data/events.db), AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN (required, no default), and LOG_LEVEL (default info) from environment variables at startup
fail to start and emit a descriptive error message
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-zero process exit; descriptive error message written to stderr or structured log output
acceptance_criteria.state: AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN is absent from the environment at startup

acceptance_criteria.action: fail to start and emit a descriptive error message
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: non-zero process exit; descriptive error message written to stderr or structured log output
acceptance_criteria.state: LOG_LEVEL is set to an invalid value at startup

user_story.goal: consume structured JSON log output from basic_svc
user_story.benefit: logs can be ingested and queried by log aggregation pipelines such as Fluentd or Loki

acceptance_criteria.action: emit all log entries as JSON objects to support log aggregation pipelines
honour the LOG_LEVEL value (debug, info, warn, error) to filter log output at runtime

requirements.title: Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM and SIGINT

user_story.goal: send SIGTERM to basic_svc during pod termination
user_story.benefit: in-flight requests complete before the process exits, avoiding dropped connections

acceptance_criteria.action: stop accepting new connections and allow in-flight requests to complete before exiting, enforcing a maximum shutdown timeout of 30 seconds
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a SIGTERM or SIGINT signal is received

acceptance_criteria.action: exit the process regardless of pending requests
acceptance_criteria.state: the 30-second shutdown timeout expires before all in-flight requests have completed

user_story.goal: run integration tests that exercise the full HTTP-to-SQLite pipeline
user_story.benefit: regressions in ingestion, authentication, validation, and health checks are caught automatically

acceptance_criteria.action: include an integration test that sends a POST /v1/events request with a valid token and payload, asserts a 201 Created response, and verifies the event is persisted in SQLite
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: test passes; HTTP 201 verified; event row present in events table

acceptance_criteria.action: include integration tests that send POST /v1/events with missing or invalid bearer tokens and assert 401 Unauthorized responses
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: test passes; HTTP 401 verified for each invalid token scenario

acceptance_criteria.action: include integration tests that send POST /v1/events with a missing Content-Type header and with an empty body, asserting 400 Bad Request responses
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: test passes; HTTP 400 verified for each invalid payload scenario

acceptance_criteria.action: include integration tests for GET /healthz and GET /readyz under normal operating conditions, asserting 200 OK responses
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: test passes; HTTP 200 verified for both health check endpoints

correctness_properties.title: Authenticated requests are always persisted
correctness_properties.for_any: POST /v1/events request that passes bearer token validation, Content-Type validation, and body non-emptiness validation
correctness_properties.invariant: exactly one row is inserted into the events table with the raw body stored in payload and received_at set to the current UTC time

correctness_properties.title: Unauthenticated requests never reach storage
correctness_properties.for_any: POST /v1/events request with a missing, malformed, or incorrect Authorization header
correctness_properties.invariant: no row is inserted into the events table and the response status is 401 Unauthorized

correctness_properties.title: Invalid payload requests never reach storage
correctness_properties.for_any: POST /v1/events request with a valid bearer token but a missing Content-Type: application/json header or an empty body
correctness_properties.invariant: no row is inserted into the events table and the response status is 400 Bad Request

correctness_properties.title: Readiness reflects database availability
correctness_properties.invariant: the response is 200 OK if and only if the SELECT 1 query succeeds; otherwise the response is 503 Service Unavailable

correctness_properties.title: Service startup requires AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN
correctness_properties.for_any: service startup attempt without AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN set in the environment
correctness_properties.invariant: the process exits with a non-zero status and emits a descriptive error before binding to any port

steps.action: sends POST /v1/events with Authorization: Bearer <token>, Content-Type: application/json, and a non-empty JSON body
extracts and validates the bearer token against AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN
validates that Content-Type is application/json and that the body is non-empty
inserts a new row into the events table with a generated UUID, the raw JSON payload, and the current UTC timestamp in received_at
returns HTTP 201 Created with an empty response body and no Content-Type header
sends POST /v1/events with a missing, malformed, or incorrect Authorization header
detects the missing or invalid bearer token during authentication middleware processing
returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with an Echo Framework error body and does not write to the events table
sends POST /v1/events with a valid bearer token but either no Content-Type: application/json header or an empty body
validates the Content-Type header and body size after authentication succeeds
returns HTTP 400 Bad Request with an Echo Framework error body and does not write to the events table
sends GET /healthz to verify the process is alive
returns HTTP 200 OK immediately without performing any database checks
sends GET /readyz to verify the service is ready to handle traffic
executes SELECT 1 against the SQLite database to confirm connectivity
returns HTTP 200 OK indicating the service is ready
sends GET /readyz to verify service readiness
executes SELECT 1 against the SQLite database and the query fails or the connection is unavailable
returns HTTP 503 Service Unavailable with body {"message": "service unavailable"}
sends SIGTERM to the basic_svc process to initiate pod termination
stops accepting new incoming connections
waits for in-flight requests to complete, up to a maximum of 30 seconds
exits the process cleanly once all in-flight requests are done or the timeout elapses

error_handling.condition: Content-Type header is absent or is not application/json on POST /v1/events
error_handling.behavior: Return 400 Bad Request with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json; do not write to the events table

error_handling.condition: Request body is empty on POST /v1/events
error_handling.behavior: Return 400 Bad Request with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json; do not write to the events table

error_handling.condition: Database or internal error occurs during event storage
error_handling.behavior: Return 500 Internal Server Error with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json

error_handling.condition: Authorization header is missing on POST /v1/events
error_handling.behavior: Return 401 Unauthorized with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json; do not write to the events table

error_handling.condition: Authorization header is present but malformed (wrong prefix casing, extra whitespace, or missing 'Bearer ' prefix)
error_handling.behavior: Return 401 Unauthorized with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json; do not write to the events table

error_handling.condition: Authorization header is well-formed but the token value does not match AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN
error_handling.behavior: Return 401 Unauthorized with Echo Framework error body {"message": "<error description>"} and Content-Type: application/json; do not write to the events table

error_handling.condition: SELECT 1 database ping fails or connection is unavailable when GET /readyz is received
error_handling.behavior: Return 503 Service Unavailable with body {"message": "service unavailable"} and Content-Type: application/json

error_handling.condition: AUTH_BEARER_TOKEN environment variable is absent at service startup
error_handling.behavior: Exit with a non-zero status code and emit a descriptive error message before binding to any port

error_handling.condition: LOG_LEVEL environment variable is set to an unrecognised value at service startup
error_handling.behavior: Exit with a non-zero status code and emit a descriptive error message before binding to any port

error_handling.condition: Graceful shutdown timeout of 30 seconds elapses before all in-flight requests complete
error_handling.behavior: Force-exit the process regardless of pending requests
